titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/155

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=429 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:16:36.607006+00 | translation moa=430 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:16:38.104696+00

p.cl: Omnibus qui has litteras uiderint uel audierint Patricius de Ridale Salutem .

Sciatis me concessisse et hac mea Carta confirmas
se Deo et Eccelsie Sancte marie de melros et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus in liberam . puram et perpetuam Elemosinam
Donationem Roberti de Bernoldebi quam fecit prefate Ecclesie de illa parte terre in territorio de Wittun que continetur infra has di
uisas . Scilicet a capite de Harhopedene ascendendo uersus occidentem per sicum qui est diuisa inter eandem terram et illam que
dicitur terra Duarii usque ad spinam decontra pratum . et inde in transuersum uersus austrum per tractum sulci qui est diuisa inter ipsam ter
ram et illam quam Willelmus persona de Hunum tenuit ad firmam de prefato Roberto De Bernoldebi  usque ad fossam quandam
que est diuisa inter predictas terras ad australem partem . et inde deorsum uersus orientem sicut sulcus uadit inter prescriptas terras usque ad pre
dictam capud de Harhopedene . Quare uolo ut prefati monachi de melros teneant et possideant predictam terram ita libere et
quiete plene et honorifice modis omnibus sicut carta predicti Roberti . testatur et confirmat . Ita quod ego et heredes mei manutenebimus et
warantizabimus predictis monachis eandem terram contra omnes homines in perpetuum . Saluo tamen seruitio meo de predicto Roberto . et heredibus
suis .

Test’. Magistro Iohanne de Hunted’ . Thomas de Coleuill’ . Ricardo le Naim . Rogero . Burnard . Iohanne de Perndell’ .
Willelmo . Chernun . Ricardo . de Cukewald’ . Michaele de Malcarwest’ . et Multis aliis .
To all who shall see or hear these letters, Patrick of Ryedale sends greeting.

Know me to have licensed and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the Church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, in free, pure, and perpetual alms, the donation of Robert of Barnoldby which he made to the aforementioned church in respect of that portion of land in the territory of Whitton which is comprised within these boundaries, namely, from the head of ‘Harrowdean’ going up towards the west by the syke that is the division between the same land and that which is called ‘[?] the land of the Dower’, as far as the thornbush opposite the meadow, and from there across towards the south along the length of the furrow which is the division between the same land and that which William the parson of Hownam held at ferm of the aforementioned Robert of Barnodlby, as far as a fosse that is the division between the said lands to the south side, and from there downwards towards the east as the ditch goes between the aforewritten lands up to the said head at ‘Harrowdean’. Wherefore I will that the said monks of Melrose should hold and possess the said land as freely and quitely, fully and honourably by all means, just as the charter of the aforesaid Robert bears witness and affirms; so that I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant to the aforesaid monks the same land against all men for ever; saving only my service from the said Robert and his heirs.
Witness: Master John of Huntingdon; Thomas of Colville; Richard le Naym; Roger Burnard; John de Perndell; William Chernun; Richard of Coxwald; Michael of Makerstoun; and many others.
